paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-T-cell; cancer tissues / tumor microenvironment; no T cell subset
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: dualistic role of ZEB1 and ZEB2 as immune modulators and their effect on tumor microenvironment
paper_assessments.rationale: Review of ZEB1/ZEB2 in EMT and cancer prognosis; only generic immune-modulator mention, no TCR inhibition evidence and cannot support tiers.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human scRNA-seq and murine; CD8+ tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (effector T cells)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: ZEB2 may drive CD8+ differentiation along the cytotoxic effector trajectory in NSCLC tumors
paper_assessments.rationale: ZEB2 promotes cytotoxic effector differentiation and anti-tumor immunity, opposing dysfunction; this is effector differentiation, not a TCR inhibition experiment.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: circUBAP2(9,12) inhibits NPC invasion and metastasis by down-regulating ZEB2 through competitive binding to AUF1
paper_assessments.rationale: ZEB2 studied in cancer cell EMT/metastasis; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (murine); primary CD8+ effector and memory T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Zeb2 promotes the terminal differentiation of CD8+ effector and memory T cell populations
paper_assessments.rationale: Zeb2 controls CD8+ terminal differentiation during infection; generic differentiation, not TCR signalling inhibition or an inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: ZEB2 promoted the K48-linked ubiquitination of TCF7L2
paper_assessments.rationale: ZEB2 modulates TCF7L2 in microglia polarization during stroke; non-T-cell system, unrelated to TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: ZEB1 and ZEB2 play functionally distinct roles in CD8+ T cell differentiation
paper_assessments.rationale: Addresses ZEB1/ZEB2/miR-200 in CD8+ T cell fate/differentiation; no inhibitory phenotype or TCR attenuation readout.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human samples and murine; placental trophoblast cells (non-immune)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Reduced ZEB2 contributes to pre-eclampsia by activating the Wnt/β-Catenin pathway
paper_assessments.rationale: ZEB2 studied in trophoblast biology and pre-eclampsia; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Inducible TCR expression reduced surface TCRs and hampered function
paper_assessments.rationale: TCR mispairing/expression engineering study with no ZEB2 involvement; not about regulator-driven TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human tumor cells with T cell co-culture and syngeneic murine model; tumor-infiltrating T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: ZEB2-mediated PD-L1 upregulation on tumor cells inhibited T cell activation and cytokine secretion in a co-culture system
paper_assessments.rationale: ZEB2 (tumor-intrinsic) cooperates with SP1 to upregulate PD-L1, suppressing T cell activation and TIL activity; checkpoint-mediated inhibition but not T-cell-intrinsic proximal TCR.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: TCR-based cancer immunotherapy was enhanced by engineering T cells to express a costimulatory chimeric antigen receptor
paper_assessments.rationale: Costimulatory CAR engineering to improve TCR therapy; no ZEB2 involvement and no inhibitory phenotype.

parsed.tier_rationale: One primary study (PMC12456470) provides causal/perturbational evidence that ZEB2 upregulates the PD-L1 inhibitory checkpoint and functionally suppresses T cell activation, cytokine secretion, and TIL activity. However, ZEB2 acts tumor-cell-intrinsically via PD-L1 rather than within T cells, so it is not a direct proximal TCR-inhibition experiment and mechanistic directness on TCR signalling is incomplete. Remaining ZEB2 T-cell papers address only differentiation/effector fate (generic, insufficient), and other papers are non-T-cell systems, reviews, or unrelated TCR-engineering. This meets moderate (indirect mechanistic/checkpoint link) but not strong; applying the lower-tier rule.
